Ok. Let's get real. Russia has 100,000 "troops" spread across three zones. Of that at least 1/4 to 1/3 are going to be support troops. That leaves at most 75,000 combat troops...scattered across three zones too far apart to be mutually supporting except in the broadest sense. Within that, break that number into the crews of vehicles and heavy weapons systems, including anti-aircraft that doesn't directly engage on the ground. (4 crewman per tank, 3 per APC, 2 per truck, 6 per artillery piece + command staff, 6 per AA system + command staff, etc, etc, etc) So now we've cut the number again. factor in a huge portion of the force is 1yr conscripts. That's how you boil it down into actual combat "units" or assets capable of engaging in ground combat. The actual "troop" numbers spiral down quickly and get spread pretty thin. Last count of identified Battalion Tactical Groups in the area is 73-75. Each one consisting of 1,000 guys spread out across all those crewed assets. That boils down to 25 BTGs per sector. Not enough to force decisive breakthroughs even if they come in simultaneously from three directions. Then factor in notoriously unreliable Russian motorized assets. And they'd be operating on extended lines, into enemy territory, away from fixed motor pools. Breakdowns are going to sideline 25% or more in the first week. Russians def do cold weather, but try swapping out a blown tank transmission in the field in -25 weather. Arrayed against that is a Ukrainian army that's had a decade to get it's shit together and now numbers 250,000. You've also got them actively arming civilian reserves to act as guerillas. These civilians are highly nationalistic at this point. (This isn't Donbas with majority ethnic Russians.) The areas of consequence that any Russian advance would have to move into are post Soviet, urbanized, concrete, jungles. Little Stalingrad strong points every step of the way with furious Ukrainians (pop. 43 million) lighting you up from every rooftop, basement, and sewer pipe. Woe be to the poor BTG that steps into that hornets nest. They will get eaten alive. As that shit show unfolds there's gonna be Ukrainian SF / Partizan units jumping into Belarus causing mayhem in the rear, throwing a push into Crimea, etc. Pick a point where you want to cause chaos and draw troops away from the main offensives. Sky's the limit. This whole thing is bluster and BS. This nonsense from Miley the other day about Kiev falling in 72hrs is pure BS. He's either the dumbest general ever or playing his role in the corp machine. (likely both) If anyone is "Colluding" with Russians it's the Byden cabal to provide a huge distraction for their failing clusterfk domestically. OR...they're being sucked into this imbroglio by a newly minted Sino-Russian alliance in order to further destabilize the US. Take your pick.
